titleStmt.title: SII 9.58: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 58. (A.R. No. 272 of 1918.) ON ANOTHER HERO-STONE IN THE SAME PLACE.

summary.p: This record is of the same date as the previous one and belongs to the reign of the same king. Here also Bijja, Nityavarsha's Mahāsāmanta, is said to have been ruling over Māsiyavāḍi hundred-and-forty and Kōgaḷi five-hundred. A son of Nākalokka of the Kadamba dynasty is said to have died victorious after a fight with Aṇṇiga, son of Ayyappa of Noḻambavāḍi, who had attacked Paḍugallu.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . nityavarṣadēva śrīprithuvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja pa
ramēsvara paramabhaṭṭāraka pravarddhamāna vijayaśrīinda rabhallahana rājya
muttarōttaramabhivṛddhige saluttire sakhanṛpakālātītasaṁ
vatsaraśataṅgaḷeṇṭunūṟa nālvattanālkaneya citrabhānu
vemba saṁvatsaraṁ pravartise svasti samadhigatapañcamahā
śabda mahāsāvanta birudaraṟoṭṭa birudara maikutti śrīmat bijja
māsiyavāḍi nūṟanālvattu kōgaḷiyaynūṟuvanāḷuttire svasti samastabhuvanabhāskaracaraṇāravṛndayuguḷa banavāsipuraparamēsvara mṛgēndralāñchana vānaradhvaja Asvamēdhāvabhṛthasa bhīma baddekandarpa bhṛtyacintāmaṇi
nannimārtaṁḍa kadaṁbavamśōdbhavaṁ śrīmat kaṇnavāra nākalokkana maganum ga
noḻaṁbavāḍiyayyappadēvana maganappaṇnigan pari bandu paḍugalla muttidoḍe kōlaṁbu pāṟidoḍe maṇnina
noḻaṁbavāḍiyayyapadēvana maganaṇnigaṁ srāvaṇa suddhabhādrapada māsada bahaḷa (?) puṇni meyumaṅgaravāradaṁdu
kālāḷkāduttire suṅgalaṁbu pāṟidoḍe māṇenendu barkkaṁbi ḷiḻdu bandu pariya kādi geldu suragiṟidu svarggata
nādo ©
Digital edition of SII 9.58 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
